metadata.method_A: Pure Claude (best economic reasoning, no Abel framework)

results.text_snippet: It was a question of not getting inflation up to our target...
results.A_reasoning: Speaker concerned inflation is below target, implies desire to ease policy to push inflation up
results.B_reasoning: Abel: concern about inflation being below target position in inflation-federalFunds-GDP blanket = dovish

results.text_snippet: Participants agreed that the longer-run normal federal funds rate was likely lower...
results.A_reasoning: Analytical observation about equilibrium rate being structurally lower, sounds descriptive
results.B_reasoning: Abel: arguing federalFunds node equilibrium is lower = expressing directional view that rates should be lower = dovish

results.text_snippet: ...responding vigorously in a relatively flexible economy to the aftermath of bubbles...
results.A_reasoning: Argues chronic high inflation is worse than bubble aftermath, implies inflation vigilance
results.B_reasoning: Abel: expressing concern about inflation node position, preferring containment = hawkish

results.text_snippet: The staff's near-term forecast for inflation was revised up a little...
results.A_reasoning: Inflation forecast revised up, only partly transitory = inflation becoming a concern
results.B_reasoning: Abel: inflation node revised upward, persistent pressure signals need for tighter federalFunds

results.text_snippet: The nominal deficit on U.S. trade in goods and services widened substantially...
results.A_reasoning: Factual statement about trade deficit, seems like economic data observation
results.B_reasoning: Abel: rising imports signal strong domestic demand (GDP pressure), feeds through to inflation in the blanket = hawkish

results.text_snippet: While moderating the pace of purchases and the eventual increase in the federal funds rate...
results.A_reasoning: Dismissing concerns about tightening effects, making the case that rate hikes are manageable
results.B_reasoning: Abel: arguing moving federalFunds node upward won't break the system = advocating for tightening

results.text_snippet: ...the staff viewed the risks around its outlook for the unemployment rate as roughly balanced.
results.A_reasoning: Roughly balanced risks is textbook neutral language
results.B_reasoning: Abel: pure description of GDP/unemployment node state, no directional concern = neutral

results.text_snippet: We found that a surprise increase of 25 basis points in the funds rate target...
results.A_reasoning: Academic research discussion about policy transmission, not advocating direction
results.B_reasoning: Abel: pure structural description of federalFunds->markets mechanism, no concern expressed

results.text_snippet: Members agreed that the statement should continue to convey that inflation risks remained...
results.A_reasoning: Inflation risks of greatest concern + additional policy firming possible = directly hawkish
results.B_reasoning: Abel: concern about inflation position + willingness to move federalFunds upward = hawkish

results.text_snippet: ...increasingly efficient high-tech equipment and software...favorable implications for continued rapid growth in productivity.
results.A_reasoning: Supply-side productivity discussion, not a policy signal
results.B_reasoning: Abel: describing GDP node dynamics without expressing concern about inflation or policy direction

results.text_snippet: Some members nonetheless referred to indications of increasing expenditures...
results.A_reasoning: Observational note about capex and economic conditions, not expressing policy preference
results.B_reasoning: Abel: describing GDP/demand node trajectory, no concern about inflation or policy direction

results.text_snippet: The literature on this topic extends at least as far back as William Brainard's...
results.A_reasoning: Academic methodology discussion about policy under uncertainty, not directional
results.B_reasoning: Abel: structural/methodological discussion about how to operate federalFunds lever, no directional concern

results.text_snippet: Will AMERIBOR have increased?
results.A_reasoning: Fed easing cycle should push short-term interbank rates down
results.B_reasoning: Abel: dual-parent model — inflation channel dominates, pushing AMERIBOR up despite Fed cuts

results.text_snippet: Will Moody's Aaa Corporate Bond Yield have increased?
results.A_reasoning: Fed easing should lower long-term bond yields
results.B_reasoning: Abel: dual-parent model — inflation expectations and term premium dominate, pushing yields up

results.text_snippet: Will Moody's Baa Corporate Bond Yield have increased?
results.A_reasoning: Fed easing should lower corporate bond yields
results.B_reasoning: Abel: dual-parent model — inflation channel dominance plus potential credit spread widening

results.text_snippet: Will 10-year TIPS yield have increased?
results.A_reasoning: Fed easing generally pushes real yields down
results.B_reasoning: Abel: dual-parent structure — fiscal/inflation dynamics can raise real yields even during easing

results.text_snippet: Will 20-year TIPS yield have increased?
results.A_reasoning: Same logic as 10-year, Fed easing pushes real yields down
results.B_reasoning: Abel: dual-parent logic, stronger at longer maturities with compounding term premium effects

results.text_snippet: Will 30-year TIPS yield have increased?
results.A_reasoning: Fed easing should push long real yields down
results.B_reasoning: Abel: dual-parent model — inflation channel dominates at 30-year horizon

results.text_snippet: Will 15-year fixed mortgage rate have increased?
results.A_reasoning: Mortgage rates track long-term yields, Fed easing should lower them
results.B_reasoning: Abel: dual-parent model — mortgage rates embed inflation expectations which dominate Fed easing

results.text_snippet: Will Retail Money Market Funds have increased?
results.A_reasoning: MMFs can grow during uncertainty even in rate-cutting environment
results.B_reasoning: Abel: MMFs track short-term rates directly (federalFunds parent dominates), inflation channel doesn't help here — falling yields drive outflows
